mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline show no significant outperformance vs. baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates signal deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate earnings visibility and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant underperformance during UNRATE spikes (> 0.5pp 3m) or t-test p > 0.05 vs. baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and housing starts indicates strong aggregate demand, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant positive correlation with combined GDPC1/HOUST growth or interaction term IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving supply-side capacity utilization, reducing inflationary pressures and enabling positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% erodes real returns, prompting central bank tightening that depresses equity valuations and causes negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant underperformance during CPIAUCSL > 2.5% periods or t-test p > 0.05 vs. baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP growth signal weak demand, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant negative correlation with combined GDPC1/RSAFS decline or interaction term IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth exceeding 150k signals robust labor market conditions, increasing consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant outperformance during PAYEMS > 150k periods or t-test p > 0.05 vs. baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening cycle characterized by rising FEDFUNDS and falling CPIAUCSL indicates disinflationary monetary policy, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.3pp and CPIAUCSL falls > 0.2pp in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL interaction term or IC < 0.05.
